headline: KAEOS's incident-priority logic matches a real ServiceNow instance on 100% of 5000 incidents; invoice late-payment prediction beats its baseline on 2,466 real settled invoices; HR attrition-risk runs well above chance on a rare event. Weaker domains are reported honestly, including one dataset whose label carries no learnable signal.
note: KAEOS deterministic classifiers (the rule-based safety net the agents fall back to when the model is weak) scored against recorded human outcomes on real Kaggle enterprise datasets. No LLM in the loop — fully reproducible. Raw data gitignored; see kaggle_ref.

hr_attrition.ground_truth: Attrition (Yes/No) — did this employee actually leave
hr_attrition.interpretation: DECENT — balanced accuracy well above chance on a rare (16%) event; catches flight risk without flooding the queue. Raw accuracy trails the always-say-'no' baseline, as expected for a rare-positive flag.

support_priority.ground_truth: Ticket Priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low) assigned by support staff
support_priority.interpretation: NO SIGNAL — this dataset's priority is synthetic: ~25% per class, flat across every ticket type. 25% exact IS the ceiling for a random 4-class label. Within-one-level (74%) is the only meaningful read. Kept for transparency.

incident_priority.ground_truth: priority (1-Critical..4-Low) + made_sla — a real ServiceNow instance
incident_priority.interpretation: STRONG — KAEOS's ITIL impact×urgency matrix reproduces the real ServiceNow priority exactly. This is production logic, not a heuristic.

sales_conversion.ground_truth: converted_within_90_days — did the lead actually convert
sales_conversion.interpretation: WEAK — deterministic engagement scoring trails baseline; conversion here depends on features the rule set doesn't weigh.

procurement_compliance.interpretation: NO SIGNAL — the Compliance label is ~82% 'Yes' uniformly across every order status and feature; it is not learnable by any classifier. Baseline is the ceiling. Kept for transparency.

finance_late_payment.ground_truth: DaysLate > 0 — was the invoice actually settled after its due date
finance_late_payment.interpretation: STRONG — customer payment history + dispute status beats the majority baseline by a wide margin (81% vs 64%), with calibrated confidence: the model is measurably more confident on the invoices it gets right. This is the AR collections agent's chase/don't-chase call on real settled invoices.

legal_clause_type.ground_truth: Clause category assigned by Atticus Project legal experts (CUAD v1)
legal_clause_type.interpretation: PARTIAL — ~40% exact over 36 expert-labelled clause categories (chance is ~3%, majority class ~8%). Phrase rules nail the unmistakable clauses (insurance, escrow, governing law) and return Unclassified at low confidence for the rest, which routes them to a human — exactly the HITL contract. The LLM tier exists for the remaining 60%.
